meta.title: PromptEngines Dashboard
meta.subtitle: Applied AI lab control surface
meta.deployment_note: Built inside pantheon-os for speed. Intended to migrate into the PromptEngines repo later.

surfaces.name: Public products
surfaces.description: Customer-facing and public-facing surfaces currently shaping PromptEngines.

surfaces.name: Internal systems
surfaces.description: Systems that make the lab operable as a human-led, agent-operated company.

now: Reframe Pantheon OS as a generic company operating system with PromptEngines as first deployment.
Build the PromptEngines dashboard MVP as a migration-ready surface inside this repo.
Design the operating terminal / terminal manager as the tactical command center.
Create metaphors, diagrams, and definitions for the pantheon model, sub-agents, capability sets, and scaling assumptions.

build_stream.title: Pantheon OS repo reframed around dashboard, terminal, and runtime
Two workstreams defined: PromptEngines dashboard MVP and terminal manager MVP
Inverted pyramid org, capability sets, and scaling thesis documented
PromptEngines dashboard implementation begins in migration-ready folder

panels.title: Why this exists
panels.body: PromptEngines needs a clean strategic dashboard surface before the terminal manager goes live. This dashboard makes the company legible while Pantheon OS evolves into a deployable company operating system.

panels.title: Migration plan
panels.body: This folder is intentionally standalone: static assets + HTML + JS + JSON. It can be moved into the PromptEngines repo later with minimal translation.

panels.title: What comes after
panels.body: Once the dashboard is credible, the operating terminal adds the live control plane: chats, terminals, controllers, agent panes, summaries, and interventions.
